incidents.title: OVHcloud SBG2 Data Center Fire
incidents.description: Fire destroyed one data center building (SBG2) and damaged another (SBG1) at OVHcloud's Strasbourg campus. Approximately 3.6 million websites went offline, affecting customers across Europe including French government portals and game servers.
incidents.impact: SBG2 destroyed entirely; SBG1 partially damaged. ~3.6M websites offline. Recovery took weeks.

incidents.title: Equinix SY4 Sydney Power Outage
incidents.description: Extended power supply failure caused outages at Equinix's SY4 data center in Sydney. Multiple enterprise customers experienced service disruption.
incidents.impact: Service disruption for multiple enterprise customers. Hours of downtime.

incidents.title: Microsoft Azure West Europe Cooling Failure
incidents.description: Record European heatwave caused cooling system failures at Azure data centers in UK and Netherlands. Microsoft was forced to shut down portions of infrastructure to prevent hardware damage.
incidents.impact: Azure, Teams, Exchange Online degraded for customers across Europe.
incidents.source_note: Microsoft Azure status page, July 2022; BBC News

incidents.title: Google Cloud / Oracle UK Cooling Failure
incidents.description: Same 2022 UK heatwave (40°C+) caused cooling failures at Google Cloud and Oracle Cloud facilities in the UK. Google declared a major incident; Oracle Cloud suffered outages.
incidents.impact: Google Cloud services degraded; Oracle Cloud UK outage.

incidents.title: Stuxnet Attack on Natanz Enrichment Facility
incidents.description: Stuxnet worm caused centrifuges at Iran's Natanz enrichment facility to spin out of control while reporting normal to operators. First known cyber weapon to cause physical destruction of industrial equipment.
incidents.impact: ~1,000 centrifuges destroyed. Setback Iran's nuclear program by estimated 1-2 years.
incidents.source_note: Langner (2011); Sanger NYT; widely documented academic research

incidents.title: Ukraine Power Grid Cyberattack
incidents.description: BlackEnergy malware attack caused three Ukrainian regional power distribution companies to lose power, affecting approximately 225,000 customers. First confirmed cyberattack to cause a power outage.
incidents.impact: 225,000 customers without power for 1-6 hours. Multiple substations taken offline.
incidents.source_note: SANS ICS; E-ISAC report; Kim Zetter, Wired 2016

incidents.title: Colonial Pipeline Ransomware Attack
incidents.description: DarkSide ransomware infected Colonial Pipeline's IT systems, causing the company to shut down 5,500 miles of fuel pipeline as a precaution. Largest cyberattack on US critical infrastructure at the time.
incidents.impact: Fuel shortages across US East Coast. $4.4M ransom paid. Pipeline offline 6 days.
incidents.source_note: CISA advisory AA21-131A; DOJ press release May 2021

incidents.title: Shamoon Attack on Saudi Aramco
incidents.description: Shamoon (Disttrack) malware wiped approximately 35,000 workstations at Saudi Aramco, rendering them inoperable. One of the most destructive cyberattacks on critical infrastructure to date.
incidents.impact: ~35,000 workstations wiped. Oil production temporarily disrupted. Operations restored after 10+ days.
incidents.source_note: Symantec; US Defense Secretary Panetta speech; multiple public reports

incidents.title: TRITON/TRISIS ICS Malware Attack
incidents.target: Petro Rabigh / Middle East Petrochemical Facility
incidents.description: TRITON malware targeted safety instrumented systems (SIS/Triconex) at a Saudi petrochemical plant, attempting to disable safety systems that prevent physical explosions. Discovery was accidental — malware bug caused process shutdown.
incidents.impact: Plant emergency shutdown triggered. If undetected, could have caused catastrophic explosion. First malware designed to attack SIS.

incidents.title: NotPetya Global Wiper Attack
incidents.description: NotPetya wiper malware spread from Ukrainian accounting software MeDoc, rapidly destroying systems at Maersk, Merck, FedEx, hospitals, and others worldwide. Caused an estimated $10 billion in damage.
incidents.impact: $10B estimated global damage. Maersk replaced ~45,000 PCs and 4,000 servers. Most destructive cyberattack in history.
incidents.source_note: US/UK attribution statement Feb 2018; Wired (Greenberg); multiple government reports

incidents.title: Texas Winter Storm Uri Data Center Outages
incidents.target: Multiple Texas data centers (Rackspace, CyrusOne, QTS)
incidents.description: Winter Storm Uri caused unprecedented power grid failures across Texas. Multiple Dallas-area data centers experienced extended power outages as ERCOT grid failed.
incidents.impact: Extended outages at Rackspace, CyrusOne, QTS. Millions of Texans without power. Data centers relied on generators for days.
incidents.source_note: Data Center Dynamics; ERCOT outage reporting, February 2021

incidents.title: Cyberattack on Shahid Rajaei Port, Iran
incidents.description: Cyberattack on the control systems of Shahid Rajaei port caused traffic management systems to malfunction, creating significant disruption to shipping operations. Widely attributed as retaliation for Iranian cyberattack on Israeli water infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Port traffic management disrupted. Shipping delays. Systems down several days.
incidents.source_note: Washington Post, May 2020; multiple intelligence sources

incidents.title: Iranian Cyberattack on Israeli Water Infrastructure
incidents.target: Israeli Water Authority / regional water facilities
incidents.description: Iranian-linked hackers attempted to increase chlorine levels in Israeli water treatment facilities. Attack was detected and blocked. Marked a major escalation in cyber-physical conflict in the region.
incidents.impact: Attack blocked before physical damage. Prompted Israeli cyber retaliation (Shahid Rajaei port).
incidents.source_note: Ynet News; Washington Post; Israeli Water Authority statement April/May 2020

incidents.title: Iran IRGC Drone Strikes on AWS Data Centers (UAE & Bahrain)
incidents.target: Amazon Web Services — UAE and Bahrain regions (3 facilities)
incidents.description: Iranian IRGC drone strikes directly hit three Amazon Web Services data center facilities: two in the UAE (Abu Dhabi region) and one in Bahrain. IRGC state media claimed the facilities were supporting U.S. military and intelligence networks. Strikes caused fires, structural damage, and water damage from firefighting. First known military strike against a major American hyperscaler's cloud infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Three AWS facilities taken offline. Regional banking disrupted (Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank, Emirates NBD, First Abu Dhabi Bank). Payments platforms (Hubpay, Alaan), enterprise services (Snowflake), ride-hailing (Careem), and Amazon delivery services affected across the Gulf region.
incidents.source_note: AP News, CNBC, Bloomberg, Fortune — March 2026

incidents.title: Oldsmar Water Treatment Plant Attack
incidents.description: Attacker remotely accessed Oldsmar water treatment plant via TeamViewer and increased sodium hydroxide (lye) levels to 111x normal (from 100ppm to 11,100ppm). Operator noticed and reversed change in real time.
incidents.impact: Attack blocked by alert operator. If undetected, could have poisoned drinking water for 15,000 residents.
incidents.source_note: FBI/CISA/MS-ISAC joint advisory; Pinellas County Sheriff press conference, February 2021

incidents.title: AISURU/Kimwolf/JackSkid IoT Botnet Disruption — Record 31.4 Tbps DDoS
incidents.actor: Jacob Butler (Canada) + unknown operators — AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad botnets
incidents.target: Global critical infrastructure, DoD networks, 3M+ IoT devices
incidents.description: DOJ, Canadian, and German authorities disrupted four of the world's largest IoT DDoS botnets (AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) responsible for record-breaking attacks. The Kimwolf botnet alone infected 1.8 million devices and issued 1.7 billion DDoS commands in November 2025, peaking at 31.4 Tbps — the largest DDoS attack ever recorded. Lumen null-routed ~1,000 C2 servers. Attackers exploited DVRs, webcams, routers, and Android smart TVs. DoD networks were among targets.
incidents.impact: 3M+ infected devices globally. Record 31.4 Tbps peak attack (35 seconds). DoD networks targeted. C2 infrastructure seized. No arrests at time of disruption.
incidents.source_note: DOJ press release, The Hacker News, Krebs on Security — March 20, 2026
